title: Variational Monte Carlo study of a two-orbital Hubbard model for the iron pnictides
authors: Vito Marino, Gabriele Gatti, Massimo Capone, Luca F. Tocchio
abstract: We study a two-orbital Hubbard-Kanamori model, which has been originally proposed for iron-based superconductors, using variational Monte Carlo. We span the nonmagnetic sector at both hole-doping and electron-doping, with respect to the half-filled case $n=2$. We report the presence of a superconductive region with a $s^{\pm}$ symmetry only when the half-filled system is in a Mott state, while orbital selectivity is absent. These results are qualitatively different from what was reported in the three-orbital Hubbard-Kanamori model, where a more extended superconductive region was observed with a concomitant development of orbital selectivity, and they are to some extent more reminiscent of the single-band Hubbard model.
summary: This paper uses Variational Monte Carlo simulations on a two-orbital Hubbard model to study superconductivity in iron pnictides. The findings demonstrate that $s^{\pm}$ pairing emerges only when the system is a Mott insulator, and importantly, this simplified model lacks the orbital selectivity seen in more complex theoretical treatments.
main_problem: The study investigates superconductivity and electronic correlations within iron pnictides using a simplified two-orbital Hubbard model.
main_result: Superconductivity with $s^{\pm}$ symmetry is found in the two-orbital model, but only when the parent compound is a Mott insulator. Crucially, this model shows no evidence of orbital selectivity or nematicity.
method: The research employs the Variational Monte Carlo (VMC) technique using correlated variational wavefunctions to minimize the system's energy.
model_or_system: The physical system is modeled by a two-orbital Hubbard-Kanamori model, which simplifies the full five-$d$ orbital structure relevant to iron pnictides. The Hamiltonian includes local Hubbard and Hund's couplings.
key_observables: Optimal intra-orbital BCS pairing parameters ($\Delta_{\alpha,\delta}$), superconducting order parameter $\phi^2_\alpha$, and the symmetry of the gap in k-space ($\Delta_{\alpha,k}$).
important_parameters: The critical Coulomb repulsion strength $U_c \sim 3.5$ eV marks the onset of Mott insulating behavior at half filling ($n=2$). Doping away from half-filling is also a key variable.
main_assumptions: The analysis neglects inter-orbital pairing amplitudes and triplet pairing amplitudes, assuming they are negligible based on prior work; it also assumes rotational invariance for $U'$.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the emergence of finite BCS parameters correlating with Mott insulating states (Fig. 1), show the dome structure of $\phi^2_\alpha$ vs. doping (Fig. 3), and confirm $s\pm$-wave symmetry in k-space (Fig. 4).
paper_structure: The paper establishes the model, applies VMC to calculate pairing correlations across different doping regimes relative to half-filling, compares results against single-band and three-orbital models, and concludes by mapping out the superconducting dome structure.
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter strongly correlated physics, the use of advanced quantum simulation techniques like VMC for complex many-body Hamiltonians provides methodological insights relevant to simulating interacting quantum systems in general.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Upper panel: Optimal intra-orbital BCS parameters in the orbital dyz, see Eq. (5), as a function of U at half filling. Lower panel: Same as in the upper panel, but for the dxz orbital. BCS parameters are computed with the variational approach on a L = 16 × 16 lattice at J/U = 0.2.

FIG. 2. Upper panel: Optimal intra-orbital BCS parameters in the orbital dyz, see Eq. (5), as a function of n at U = 4 eV. Lower panel: Same as in the upper panel, but for the dxz orbital. BCS parameters are computed with the variational approach on a L = 16 × 16 lattice at J/U = 0.2.

FIG. 3. Square of the superconducting order parameter ϕ2 α in each orbital as a function of n at U = 4 eV (full symbols) and at U = 0.5 (empty symbols). Data are obtained on the L = 16 × 16 cluster at J/U = 0.2.

FIG. 4. Fourier transform of the optimal BCS parameter, as defined in Eq. (7), for U = 4 eV and n = 2.2, corresponding to the peak of the superconducting order parameter in the electron-doped regime. Both orbitals, dxz (upper panel) and dyz (lower panel), display an s±-wave symmetry.

FIG. 5. Electronic density per orbital nα as a function of n, for U = 4 eV. Data are obtained for J/U = 0.2. and they do not show any size dependence when moving from L = 12×12 to L = 16 × 16 clusters.
